### Data Cleaning and Preprocessing for R model (Bayesian)

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(lubridate)
library(readr)

# Load data
df <- read_csv("accidents_sample.csv")

# Select relevant columns
cols_to_keep <- c(
  "Start_Time", "Severity", "Temperature(F)",
  "Humidity(%)", "Visibility(mi)", "Weather_Condition",
  "Sunrise_Sunset", "State", "Distance(mi)"
)

df <- df[, cols_to_keep]

# Drop rows missing critical columns (Start_Time or Severity)
df <- df %>%
  filter(!is.na(Start_Time), !is.na(Severity))

# Fill numeric columns with medians
num_cols <- c("Temperature(F)", "Humidity(%)", "Visibility(mi)", "Distance(mi)")

for (col in num_cols) {
  col_median <- median(df[[col]], na.rm = TRUE)
  df[[col]][is.na(df[[col]])] <- col_median
}

# Fill categorical with mode
get_mode <- function(x) {
  tbl <- table(x, useNA = "no")
  names(tbl)[which.max(tbl)]
}

df$Weather_Condition[is.na(df$Weather_Condition)] <- get_mode(df$Weather_Condition)
df$Sunrise_Sunset[is.na(df$Sunrise_Sunset)]       <- get_mode(df$Sunrise_Sunset)

# Parse timestamps
df$Start_Time <- parse_date_time(df$Start_Time, orders = c("ymd HMS", "ymd HMSz"))
df$Date      <- as.Date(df$Start_Time)
df$Year      <- year(df$Start_Time)
df$Month     <- month(df$Start_Time)
df$Hour      <- hour(df$Start_Time)
# 0 = Monday, 6 = Sunday
df$DayOfWeek <- wday(df$Start_Time, week_start = 1) - 1

# Encode categorical variables as factors
df$Severity         <- factor(df$Severity, levels = 1:4, ordered = TRUE)
df$Weather_Condition <- factor(df$Weather_Condition)
df$Sunrise_Sunset   <- factor(df$Sunrise_Sunset)
df$State            <- factor(df$State)

# Describe data
cat(sprintf("Rows: %d | Cols: %d\n\n", nrow(df), ncol(df)))
cat("Severity distribution:\n")
print(table(df$Severity))

# Save cleaned data to use
write_csv(df %>% mutate(
  Start_Time = as.character(Start_Time),
  Date       = as.character(Date),
  Severity   = as.integer(Severity)
), "accidents_clean.csv")

```
